title: eremophila rostrata subsp. rostrata
intro: Eremophila rostrata subsp. rostrata, also known as Emu Bush, is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its clusters of small, tubular flowers and grey-green foliage. The Emu Bush is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Eremophila rostrata subsp. rostrata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
